title: How pclmbayes compares with the ungroup package
text: pclmbayes popular ungroup package implement penalised composite link models (PCLM) redistributing wide-bin counts onto fine grid. share high-level idea — B-splines plus difference penalty plus composite-link C matrix — implement different statistical models different likelihoods, links, identifiability conventions, supported bin layouts. vignette spells differences , compares three algorithms shipped pclmbayes (pclm(), bpclm(), pclm_exact()) ungroup::pclm() synthetic data known truth two real datasets distributed package.
code: library(pclmbayes)

title: How pclmbayes compares with the ungroup package
text: short: ungroup Poisson smoother grouped counts, pclmbayes multinomial density estimator Bayesian variant exact-ungrouping option. two solve overlapping distinct problems; numerical results agree closely shape fitted distribution, disagree O(shrinkage) bin totals.

title: How pclmbayes compares with the ungroup package
text: contrasting ungroup, recap pclmbayes entry point (use multinomial / softmax model): pclm() — frequentist penalised scoring (Newton/IRLS) maximises penalised multinomial log-likelihood. smoothing parameter τ\tau selected BIC default grid. Returns point estimate ϕ̂\hat\phi asymptotic variance-covariance matrix inverse penalised Fisher information. bpclm() — fully Bayesian: MALA proposal ϕ\phi adaptive step size targeting 0.57 acceptance, Gibbs draw τ\tau Gamma full conditional. Returns chain ϕ\phi, π\pi τ\tau. Optionally enforces unimodality, log-concavity, monotonicity rejecting proposals violate (Eq. 7 Lambert & Eilers). pclm_exact() — constrained MAP. Solves min⁡ϕ12ϕ′Pϕ\min_\phi \tfrac12\,\phi'P\phi subject Cπ(ϕ)=m/m+C\pi(\phi) = m / m_+ exactly, sequential quadratic programming Lagrangian. smoothness penalty objective; bin counts reproduced machine precision. canonical “ungrouping” point estimate. three differ trade . pclm() bpclm() trade fit--bins smoothness τ\tau; pclm_exact() hits bins exactly minimises residual roughness. bpclm() adds posterior uncertainty.

what: 3. Synthetic comparison: 5-year mortality bands → single years
title: How pclmbayes compares with the ungroup package
text: canonical demographic ungrouping use case: know truth (single-year deaths), aggregate 5-year bands, recover single-year breakdown. measure RMSE per single-year cell truth, max absolute residual wide-bin totals, runtime.
code: ages   <- 0:99 true_p <- 0.10 * dnorm(ages, mean =  2, sd =  3) +           0.06 * dnorm(ages, mean = 25, sd = 12) +           0.84 * dnorm(ages, mean = 75, sd = 12) true_p <- true_p / sum(true_p) N      <- 50000L true_yearly <- as.numeric(rmultinom(1L, size = N, prob = true_p)) year_to_band <- (ages %/% 5L) + 1L m_band <- as.numeric(tapply(true_yearly, year_to_band, sum)) wb     <- cbind(seq(0, 95, by = 5L), seq(5, 100, by = 5L))

previous_headings: 3. Synthetic comparison: 5-year mortality bands → single years
title: How pclmbayes compares with the ungroup package
code: t_pclm <- system.time(   fit_pclm <- pclm(m = m_band, wide_breaks = wb,                    a = 0, b = 100, ngrid = 100L,                    ndx = 22L, degree = 3L, penalty_order = 3L) )[["elapsed"]]  t_pclm_exact <- system.time(   fit_exact <- pclm_exact(m = m_band, wide_breaks = wb,                           a = 0, b = 100, ngrid = 100L,                           ndx = 22L, degree = 3L, penalty_order = 3L) )[["elapsed"]]  t_bpclm <- system.time(   fit_b <- bpclm(m = m_band, wide_breaks = wb,                  a = 0, b = 100, ngrid = 100L,                  ndx = 22L, degree = 3L, penalty_order = 3L,                  niter = 3000L, burnin = 1000L, adapt = 500L,                  seed = 7L) )[["elapsed"]] fit_b_cal <- calibrate(fit_b)             # exact band totals t_ungroup <- system.time(   fit_ung <- ungroup::pclm(x = wb[, 1L], y = m_band,                            nlast = 5, out.step = 1) )[["elapsed"]] ung_yearly <- as.numeric(fit_ung$fitted)  # length 100, single-year estimates

title: How pclmbayes compares with the ungroup package
text: expected pattern: pclm() — multinomial smoother — non-zero max bin residual (smoothness penalty pulls bin probabilities away empirical proportions). per-cell RMSE competitive wrong tool want exact totals. pclm_exact() bpclm() + calibrate() reproduce bin totals machine precision. right tool ungrouping. ungroup::pclm() — Poisson smoother — also tends small non-zero bin residual (Poisson PCLM smoothed fit, constrained one). RMSE values across four typically within 5–10% size data; per-cell shape dominated irreducible multinomial-within-band sampling noise order nj/6\sqrt{n_j / 6} 5-year band.
code: yhat_pclm  <- fit_pclm$pi  * sum(m_band) yhat_exact <- fit_exact$pi * sum(m_band) yhat_bpclm <- fit_b_cal$pi * sum(m_band)  rmse <- function(a, b) sqrt(mean((a - b) ^ 2))  # Wide-bin residual after re-aggregating the single-year fit band_resid <- function(yhat) {   band <- as.numeric(tapply(yhat, year_to_band, sum))   max(abs(band - m_band)) }  results <- data.frame(   algorithm   = c("pclm",          "bpclm + calibrate", "pclm_exact",                   "ungroup::pclm"),   RMSE_yearly = c(rmse(yhat_pclm,  true_yearly),                   rmse(yhat_bpclm, true_yearly),                   rmse(yhat_exact, true_yearly),                   rmse(ung_yearly, true_yearly)),   max_bin_residual = c(band_resid(yhat_pclm),                        band_resid(yhat_bpclm),                        band_resid(yhat_exact),                        band_resid(ung_yearly)),   elapsed_sec = c(t_pclm, t_bpclm, t_pclm_exact, t_ungroup) ) results #>           algorithm RMSE_yearly max_bin_residual elapsed_sec #> 1              pclm    44.79191     7.537445e+01       0.021 #> 2 bpclm + calibrate    34.84219     4.547474e-13       0.307 #> 3        pclm_exact    27.36794     1.818989e-12       0.026 #> 4     ungroup::pclm    78.29337     1.392698e+00       0.114

title: How pclmbayes compares with the ungroup package
text: Seven irregular wide bins blood-lead concentration, n=139n = 139. ground truth — visualise recovered density. Recovered density bloodlead data, four algorithms. pclm_exact reproduces seven observed counts machine precision; pclm() bpclm() shrink largest bin ~10% toward smooth fit; ungroup::pclm() also smoothed fit similar (generally smaller, owing Poisson rather multinomial likelihood) shrinkage pattern.
code: data(bloodlead) wb_b <- with(bloodlead, cbind(lower, upper))  fit_b_pclm  <- pclm(      m = bloodlead$count, wide_breaks = wb_b,                           a = 0, b = 80, ngrid = 80L, ndx = 17L,                           degree = 3L, penalty_order = 3L) fit_b_exact <- pclm_exact(m = bloodlead$count, wide_breaks = wb_b,                           a = 0, b = 80, ngrid = 80L, ndx = 17L,                           degree = 3L, penalty_order = 3L) fit_b_bayes <- bpclm(     m = bloodlead$count, wide_breaks = wb_b,                           a = 0, b = 80, ngrid = 80L, ndx = 17L,                           degree = 3L, penalty_order = 3L,                           niter = 4000L, burnin = 1000L, adapt = 500L,                           shape = "unimodal", seed = 1L) # bloodlead bins are contiguous: x = lower limits, nlast = last bin width. fit_b_ung <- ungroup::pclm(x        = bloodlead$lower,                            y        = bloodlead$count,                            nlast    = 80 - 65,   # last bin is [65, 80)                            out.step = 1) ung_density_b <- as.numeric(fit_b_ung$fitted) / sum(fit_b_ung$fitted) mids_b <- (head(fit_b_pclm$grid, -1L) + tail(fit_b_pclm$grid, -1L)) / 2 ymax_b <- max(fit_b_pclm$pi, fit_b_exact$pi, fit_b_bayes$pi,               if (have_ungroup) ung_density_b) plot(mids_b, fit_b_pclm$pi,  type = "l", lwd = 2, lty = 2,      col = "steelblue",      ylim = c(0, ymax_b),      xlab = "Blood-lead concentration (µg/dl)",      ylab = "Density mass per fine cell",      main = "bloodlead: density estimates") lines(mids_b, fit_b_exact$pi, col = "black",     lwd = 2) lines(mids_b, fit_b_bayes$pi, col = "firebrick", lwd = 2, lty = 3) if (have_ungroup) {   lines(seq(0.5, 79.5, by = 1), ung_density_b,         col = "darkgreen", lwd = 2, lty = 4)   legend("topright", bty = "n",          legend = c("pclm_exact", "pclm", "bpclm (unimodal)",                     "ungroup::pclm"),          col = c("black", "steelblue", "firebrick", "darkgreen"),          lwd = 2, lty = c(1, 2, 3, 4)) } else {   legend("topright", bty = "n",          legend = c("pclm_exact", "pclm", "bpclm (unimodal)"),          col = c("black", "steelblue", "firebrick"),          lwd = 2, lty = c(1, 2, 3)) } # How well does each fit reproduce the seven observed bin totals? band_tot <- function(pi_grid, fit_obj) {   as.numeric(fit_obj$C %*% pi_grid) * sum(fit_obj$m) } bl_compare <- data.frame(   observed       = bloodlead$count,   pclm           = round(fit_b_pclm$fitted_counts,  2),   bpclm          = round(band_tot(fit_b_bayes$pi,   fit_b_bayes),  2),   pclm_exact     = round(fit_b_exact$fitted_counts, 6),   ungroup        = if (have_ungroup) {                      # Re-aggregate ungroup's single-year output back to bins                      yhat <- as.numeric(fit_b_ung$fitted)                      bin_id <- findInterval(seq(0.5, 79.5, by = 1),                                             c(bloodlead$lower, 80),                                             rightmost.closed = TRUE)                      round(as.numeric(tapply(yhat, bin_id, sum)), 2)                    } else {                      rep(NA_real_, nrow(bloodlead))                    } ) bl_compare #>   observed  pclm bpclm pclm_exact ungroup #> 1       27 29.96 27.62         27   26.96 #> 2       71 63.63 68.85         71   70.86 #> 3       32 36.04 33.09         32   31.91 #> 4        6  8.12  7.76          6    6.09 #> 5        3  1.13  1.51          3    2.88 #> 6        0  0.11  0.14          0    0.03 #> 7        0  0.01  0.02          0    0.28

code: data(tbdeaths1907) wb_t <- with(tbdeaths1907, cbind(lower, upper))  fit_t_pclm  <- pclm(      m = tbdeaths1907$count, wide_breaks = wb_t,                           a = 0, b = 120, ngrid = 120L, ndx = 17L,                           degree = 3L, penalty_order = 3L) fit_t_exact <- pclm_exact(m = tbdeaths1907$count, wide_breaks = wb_t,                           a = 0, b = 120, ngrid = 120L, ndx = 17L,                           degree = 3L, penalty_order = 3L) fit_t_bayes <- bpclm(     m = tbdeaths1907$count, wide_breaks = wb_t,                           a = 0, b = 120, ngrid = 120L, ndx = 17L,                           degree = 3L, penalty_order = 3L,                           niter = 3000L, burnin = 1000L, adapt = 500L,                           seed = 2L) # tbdeaths1907 bins are contiguous; last bin is [100, 120). fit_t_ung <- ungroup::pclm(x        = tbdeaths1907$lower,                            y        = tbdeaths1907$count,                            nlast    = 120 - 100,                            out.step = 1) ung_density_t <- as.numeric(fit_t_ung$fitted) / sum(fit_t_ung$fitted) mids_t <- (head(fit_t_pclm$grid, -1L) + tail(fit_t_pclm$grid, -1L)) / 2 ymax_t <- max(fit_t_pclm$pi, fit_t_exact$pi, fit_t_bayes$pi,               if (have_ungroup) ung_density_t) plot(mids_t, fit_t_pclm$pi,  type = "l", lwd = 2, lty = 2,      col = "steelblue",      ylim = c(0, ymax_t),      xlab = "Age (years)",      ylab = "Density mass per single year",      main = "tbdeaths1907: density estimates") lines(mids_t, fit_t_exact$pi, col = "black",     lwd = 2) lines(mids_t, fit_t_bayes$pi, col = "firebrick", lwd = 2, lty = 3) if (have_ungroup) {   lines(seq(0.5, 119.5, by = 1), ung_density_t,         col = "darkgreen", lwd = 2, lty = 4)   legend("topright", bty = "n",          legend = c("pclm_exact", "pclm", "bpclm",                     "ungroup::pclm"),          col = c("black", "steelblue", "firebrick", "darkgreen"),          lwd = 2, lty = c(1, 2, 3, 4)) } else {   legend("topright", bty = "n",          legend = c("pclm_exact", "pclm", "bpclm"),          col = c("black", "steelblue", "firebrick"),          lwd = 2, lty = c(1, 2, 3)) }

title: How pclmbayes compares with the ungroup package
text: want smooth density don’t care preserving bin totals exactly — either pclm() (multinomial) ungroup::pclm() (Poisson) ; pick one whose model matches data-generating story. give similar shapes. want exact preservation wide-bin totals (canonical ungrouping use case) — use pclm_exact(). Neither ungroup::pclm() pclm() . want full posterior uncertainty, optionally shape constraint (unimodal, log-concave, monotonic) — use bpclm(), optionally followed calibrate() enforce exact band totals every draw. ungroup provide . overlapping misaligned wide bins — pclmbayes supports (via rectangle method bin_matrix()). ungroup::pclm() requires contiguous bins. 2D problem (e.g. age × year mortality) — ungroup provides directly (ungroup::pclm2D()). pclmbayes 1D current version. need exposure / population--risk offset — ungroup built ; pclmbayes need model rates externally.

title: Bayesian density estimation and exact ungrouping with pclmbayes
text: pclmbayes package estimates smooth latent continuous density data observed wide bins. Two paradigms supported answer two different questions. Density estimation (original paradigm Lambert Eilers, 2009). wide-bin counts treated multinomial sample underlying smooth density, recovered composite-link spline model. fitted bin counts constrained match observed bin counts exactly — smoothness penalty allowed shrink . Exact ungrouping (use case distributing wide-bin counts across fine grid — example, redistributing deaths 5-year age bands across single years age). wide-bin counts treated fixed totals fine-grid counts must sum back exactly. package provides functions . decision tree : pclm() — frequentist density estimation (BIC-selected smoothing). bpclm() — Bayesian density estimation shape constraints (unimodal, log-concave, monotonic) full posterior uncertainty. pclm_exact() — frequentist constrained MAP gives smoothest density consistent observed bin totals exactly. calibrate() — post-hoc projection fit (frequentist Bayesian) onto constraint manifold (exact bin totals). posterior_predict() — credible/prediction intervals fine-cell counts, multinomial sampling noise included appropriate.
code: library(pclmbayes)

what: Why the default fit doesn’t preserve bin totals
title: Bayesian density estimation and exact ungrouping with pclmbayes
text: Lambert–Eilers model wide-bin counts multinomial sample underlying smooth density. smoothness penalty shrinks fitted bin probabilities away empirical proportions, implied counts deviate observed ones. bloodlead data largest deviation 7.4 deaths (roughly 10% shrinkage largest bin): density-estimation use case correct behaviour — count mjm_j noisy realisation, estimating underlying rate. ungrouping use case (wide-bin counts data — say, deaths 5-year age band — want fine-grid breakdown sums back exactly), shrinkage unwanted.

title: Bayesian density estimation and exact ungrouping with pclmbayes
text: Group simulated single-year mortality counts 5-year bands ungroup back. canonical use case epidemiology demography. RMSE per year sits close irreducible multinomial-within-band sampling SD (nj/6≈20\sqrt{n_j / 6} \approx 20 2500-death band). See validation script dev/validate-ungrouping.R Monte Carlo coverage check.
code: ages <- 0:99 true_p <- 0.10 * dnorm(ages, mean =  2, sd =  3) +           0.06 * dnorm(ages, mean = 25, sd = 12) +           0.84 * dnorm(ages, mean = 75, sd = 12) true_p <- true_p / sum(true_p) N <- 50000L set.seed(42) true_yearly <- as.numeric(rmultinom(1, size = N, prob = true_p)) year_to_band <- (ages %/% 5L) + 1L m_band <- as.numeric(tapply(true_yearly, year_to_band, sum)) wb     <- cbind(seq(0, 95, by = 5), seq(5, 100, by = 5)) fit_pe <- pclm_exact(m = m_band, wide_breaks = wb,                      a = 0, b = 100, ngrid = 100L,                      ndx = 22L, degree = 3L, penalty_order = 3L) yhat <- fit_pe$pi * sum(m_band) sqrt(mean((yhat - true_yearly) ^ 2))         # RMSE per single year #> [1] 18.90452 max(abs(fit_pe$fitted_counts - m_band))      # exact preservation #> [1] 1.818989e-12

title: Bayesian density estimation and exact ungrouping with pclmbayes
text: pclm_exact() deterministic point estimate — uncertainty . Combining bpclm() calibrate() gives chain densities preserve bin totals exactly. helper posterior_predict() turns chain per-cell credible/prediction intervals. Two distinct uncertainty objects supported: type = "rate" — uncertainty smooth latent rate m+πm_+ \pi. Narrow large NN. Use want compare rates across populations. type = "predictive" (default) — uncertainty realised per-cell counts. Within band jj conditional distribution multinomial size mjm_j within-band probabilities πy/γj\pi_y / \gamma_j. Use want plausible breakdowns observed band counts. ```{r posterior-predict-plot, fig.width = 8, fig.height = 4.5, fig.cap = “Posterior predictive (left) rate (right) single-year deaths.”} op <- par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(4, 4, 2.5, 1)) plot(pp_predictive, show_bins = FALSE, main = “type = ‘predictive’ (90% PI)”, xlab = “Age (years)”, ylab = “Single-year deaths”) lines(ages, true_yearly, col = “firebrick”, lwd = 1.5, lty = 2) plot(pp_rate, show_bins = FALSE, main = “type = ‘rate’ (90% CI)”, xlab = “Age (years)”, ylab = “Expected single-year deaths”) lines(ages, true_yearly, col = “firebrick”, lwd = 1.5, lty = 2) par(op) Cumulative-deaths posterior predictive.
code: fit_b <- bpclm(m = m_band, wide_breaks = wb,                a = 0, b = 100, ngrid = 100L,                ndx = 22L, degree = 3L, penalty_order = 3L,                niter = 4000L, burnin = 1000L, adapt = 500L,                seed = 7) fit_b <- calibrate(fit_b)               # exact band totals on every draw  pp_predictive <- posterior_predict(fit_b, type = "predictive",                                     level = 0.9, seed = 7) pp_rate       <- posterior_predict(fit_b, type = "rate",                                     level = 0.9) The predictive interval (left) is wider because it includes the multinomial sampling noise; it has approximately nominal coverage (≈ 85% empirical at the 90% nominal level in our validation). The rate interval (right) is much narrower because at $N = 50000$ the smooth density is well-determined and the realised counts have substantial sampling noise.  ## Cumulative sums  Because the fine-cell draws preserve every band total exactly, any cumulative sum at a band boundary is exactly known: the credible band closes onto the truth at every multiple of 5. *Within* a band the credible band has positive width, reflecting how the band's counts could have been distributed across single years.   ``` r cdf_chain <- t(apply(pp_predictive$draws, 1L, cumsum)) cdf_mean  <- colMeans(cdf_chain) cdf_lo    <- apply(cdf_chain, 2L, quantile, probs = 0.05, names = FALSE) cdf_hi    <- apply(cdf_chain, 2L, quantile, probs = 0.95, names = FALSE)  ymax_cdf <- max(cumsum(true_yearly), cdf_hi) plot(ages, cumsum(true_yearly), type = "l", col = "grey40", lwd = 2,      ylim = c(0, ymax_cdf),      xlab = "Age (years)", ylab = "Cumulative deaths",      main = "Cumulative deaths: truth + 90% predictive band") polygon(c(ages, rev(ages)), c(cdf_lo, rev(cdf_hi)),         col = grDevices::adjustcolor("steelblue", 0.3), border = NA) lines(ages, cdf_mean, col = "black", lwd = 2) abline(v = seq(5, 95, by = 5), col = "grey90", lty = 3) legend("topleft",        legend = c("Truth", "Posterior predictive mean",                    "90% PI", "5-year band boundaries"),        col = c("grey40", "black",                 grDevices::adjustcolor("steelblue", 0.6), "grey90"),        lwd = c(2, 2, 8, 1), lty = c(1, 1, 1, 3), bty = "n")

what: Bayesian Density Estimation from Grouped Continuous Data via the Penalised Composite Link Model
title: Bayesian Density Estimation from Grouped Continuous Data via the Penalised Composite Link Model
text: Bayesian density estimation exact ungrouping grouped continuous data, via penalised composite link model pclmbayes covers two related problems: Density estimation — given wide-bin counts multinomial sample underlying continuous variable, recover smooth latent density (Lambert Eilers, 2009). Exact ungrouping — given wide-bin counts data (e.g. deaths 5-year age bands), distribute across fine grid (e.g. single years age) grid totals sum back exactly band totals. package provides: pclm() / bpclm() — frequentist (BIC) Bayesian fits Lambert–Eilers density model, optional shape constraints (unimodal, logconcave, monotonic). pclm_exact() — constrained MAP gives smoothest density consistent wide-bin counts exactly. calibrate() — post-hoc projection fit (frequentist Bayesian) onto constraint manifold; Bayesian fits, every posterior draw calibrated. posterior_predict() — per-cell credible/prediction intervals ungrouped fine-cell counts, multinomial sampling noise. Datasets bloodlead (Hasselblad et al. 1980) tbdeaths1907 (illustrative reconstruction) used Section 6 paper. full vignette walking .

title: Build the bin (composite-link) matrix C — bin_matrix
text: Element \(c_{ji}\) proportion \(\)th fine interval falls inside \(j\)th wide bin. wide-bin boundaries align fine grid, gives 0/1 entries (Section 2 Lambert Eilers, 2009). Misaligned overlapping wide bins handled rectangle method (Section 4 paper).

title: Bayesian penalised composite link model for grouped data — bpclm
text: m Numeric vector non-negative wide-bin counts (length \(J\)). wide_breaks Wide-bin boundaries; either \(J + 1\) vector (contiguous bins partitioning \((, b)\)) \(J \times 2\) matrix/data frame (lower, upper) limits. , b Lower upper limits support \((, b)\) estimate density. Defaults smallest lower limit largest upper limit wide_breaks. ngrid Number fine-grid intervals \(\). Defaults 100. ndx Number equally-spaced knot intervals \((, b)\). number B-splines ndx + degree. Default 17 (\(K = 20\) cubic B-splines, matching paper's examples). degree B-spline degree (default 3, cubic). penalty_order Order \(r\) difference penalty (default 3, paper's examples). niter Total number MCMC iterations (including burn-). Default 5000. burnin Number initial iterations discard. Default floor(niter / 5). thin Thinning interval (default 1). every thin-th post-burn-draw retained. adapt Number additional iterations adaptive tuning step size delta performed main run. Default 500. tau_init Initial value precision \(\tau\). NULL (default), BIC-selected value frequentist warm-start fit used. a_tau, b_tau Hyperparameters \(\Gamma(, b)\) hyperprior \(\tau\). Defaults 1e-4 give near- flat improper prior \(\log \tau\). delta_init Initial step size Langevin proposal. NULL, value \(1.65^2 / K^{1/3}\) Roberts Rosenthal (1998) used. target_accept Target acceptance rate adaptation (default 0.57). Sigma Optional positive-definite \(K \times K\) proposal covariance. NULL, frequentist warm-start variance-covariance matrix used. shape Optional character vector shape constraints. subset "unimodal", "logconcave" ("log-concave") "monotonic". Default NULL (unconstrained). shape_args list additional arguments shape indicators. Currently used monotonicity, shape_args = list(direction = "decreasing") ("increasing", default "either") restricts sense. phi_init Optional starting value chain \(\phi\). Defaults warm-start frequentist MLE. seed Optional integer reproducibility. verbose Logical: print progress every 10% iterations. probs Probabilities compute quantiles. cred Credible level band (default x$cred_level). quantile.bpclm, also reports lower/upper credible limits summary = "mean". add Logical: TRUE, add existing plot. density_col Colour fitted density line. band_col Fill colour credible band (alpha-blended). hist_col Fill colour histogram rectangles. hist_border Border colour histogram rectangles. xlab, ylab, main, ylim, xlim, lwd Standard graphical parameters (passed plotting methods pclm / bpclm fits). summary One "mean" (posterior mean quantile predicted density), "median", "sample" (returns full posterior sample matrix).

title: Bayesian penalised composite link model for grouped data — bpclm
text: object class "bpclm", list components: phi Posterior mean \(\phi\) (length \(K\)). phi_chain nsim x K matrix post-burn-,       post-thin draws \(\phi\). tau_chain Numeric vector post-burn-draws       \(\tau\). pi_chain nsim x matrix fine-grid probabilities       (row draw). pi Posterior mean \(\pi\) (latent grid density,       summing 1). pi_lower, pi_upper Pointwise 90% (, see       cred_level) credible-interval limits \(\pi\). cred_level Credible level used bands (default       0.90). accept Overall acceptance rate \(\phi\) step       across post-adaptation iterations. delta Final tuned step size. adapt_path Numeric vector tracking \(\delta\)       adaptation phase (diagnostics). warmstart frequentist pclm fit used warm       starting (NULL relevant arguments       supplied directly). grid, grid_mid, basis, C, m, wide_breaks, penalty_order,       a_tau, b_tau, shape, call Bookkeeping.

title: Bayesian penalised composite link model for grouped data — bpclm
text: sampler Metropolis-within-Gibbs scheme: modified Langevin-Hastings (MALA) update \(\phi\) followed Gibbs draw \(\tau\). proposal covariance \(\phi\) fixed matrix Sigma approximating posterior covariance, obtained frequentist warm-start fit (pclm) user-chosen \(\tau\). Optional shape constraints ("unimodal", "logconcave" /"monotonic") imposed rejecting proposal whose induced density violates , line Eq. (7) paper.

title: Calibrate a fitted PCLM to preserve wide-bin totals exactly — calibrate
text: Post-hoc adjustment fitted pclm bpclm object implied wide-bin counts match observed counts m exactly. wide bin \(j\), fine-grid probabilities \(\pi_i\) (\(\) bin \(j\)) multiplied \((m_j / m_+) / \gamma_j\), \(\gamma_j = (C\pi)_j\) fitted bin probability calibration. within-bin shape smooth fit preserved; small jumps bin boundaries price paid exact preservation.

title: Calibrate a fitted PCLM to preserve wide-bin totals exactly — calibrate
text: Calibration meaningful wide bins partition support without overlap, fine interval belongs exactly one wide bin. function checks errors bin assignment ambiguous (e.g. overlapping bins, fine-grid breakpoints misaligned wide-bin boundaries). bpclm objects, calibration applied separately every posterior draw posterior summaries (pi, pi_lower, pi_upper, phi) recomputed. Posterior credible intervals derived quantities (means, quantiles) recomputed calibrated chain respect constraint.

what: Constrained MAP fit: smoothest density that exactly preserves wide-bin counts — pclm_exact
title: Constrained MAP fit: smoothest density that exactly preserves wide-bin counts — pclm_exact
text: Solves equality-constrained optimisation problem $$\min_\phi \tfrac{1}{2} \phi' P \phi   \quad \text{subject } \quad   C \pi(\phi) = m / m_+,$$ \(\pi(\phi)\) softmax \(B \phi\), \(P\) \(r\)th-order difference penalty, \(C\) bin matrix, \(m / m_+\) vector observed wide-bin proportions. result smoothest density fine grid exactly reproduces wide-bin counts summed.

what: pclmbayes: Bayesian density estimation from grouped continuous data — pclmbayes-package
title: pclmbayes: Bayesian density estimation from grouped continuous data — pclmbayes-package
text: Implements penalised composite link model (PCLM) Lambert Eilers (2009) estimating smooth latent continuous density grouped (binned) observations. package provides:

title: pclmbayes: Bayesian density estimation from grouped continuous data — pclmbayes-package
text: pclm: frequentist penalised-scoring fit,     selection smoothing parameter AIC BIC. bpclm: fully Bayesian fit using modified     Langevin-Hastings sampler adaptive step size Gibbs     step penalty parameter. Optional shape-constraint priors (unimodality, log-concavity,     monotonicity), enforced rejection MCMC. Two example datasets used original paper:     bloodlead (Hasselblad, Stead Galke, 1980)     tbdeaths1907 (deaths tuberculosis     Netherlands 1907). model assumes support \((, b)\) latent random variable \(Y\) partitioned fine grid \(\) intervals equal width \(\Delta\). log-density fine grid modelled B-spline basis \(B\) \(K\) basis functions discrete \(r\)th-order difference penalty spline coefficients \(\phi\) (Eilers Marx, 1996). Letting \(\eta = B \phi\), latent grid probabilities $$\pi_i = \exp(\eta_i) / \sum_j \exp(\eta_j),$$ wide-bin probabilities \(\gamma = C \pi\) \(C\) binning matrix, observed wide-bin counts \(m\) follow multinomial distribution probabilities \(\gamma\). full methodological details, see package vignette.

title: pclmbayes: Bayesian density estimation from grouped continuous data — pclmbayes-package
text: Lambert, P. Eilers, P. H. C. (2009). Bayesian density estimation grouped continuous data. Computational Statistics Data Analysis, 53(4), 1388–1399. doi:10.1016/j.csda.2008.11.022 Eilers, P. H. C. Marx, B. D. (1996). Flexible smoothing B-splines penalties. Statistical Science, 11(2), 89–121. Eilers, P. H. C. (2007). Ill-posed problems counts, composite link model, penalized likelihood. Statistical Modelling, 7(3), 239–254. Roberts, G. O. Rosenthal, J. S. (1998). Optimal scaling discrete approximations Langevin diffusions. Journal Royal Statistical Society, Series B, 60(1), 255–268. Atchade, Y. F. Rosenthal, J. S. (2005). adaptive Markov chain Monte Carlo algorithms. Bernoulli, 11(5), 815–828.

pclmbayes 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Implements method Lambert Eilers (2009) “Bayesian density estimation grouped continuous data” (Computational Statistics Data Analysis 53(4), 1388-1399). Frequentist fit pclm(): penalised scoring BIC/AIC selection smoothing parameter. Bayesian fit bpclm(): modified Langevin-Hastings sampler rotation Cholesky frequentist warm-start variance- covariance, adaptive tuning step size, Gibbs step smoothing precision. Optional shape-constraint priors: unimodality, log-concavity monotonicity (Eq. 7 paper). Example datasets bloodlead (verbatim Hasselblad et al. 1980 / paper) tbdeaths1907 (illustrative reconstruction; see help page caveats). Vignette reproducing worked examples paper.
